# "apikey.name" this field is present if and only if the "authentication.type" is "api_key"
# "authentication.token.name" this field is present if and only if the authenticating credential is a service account token
# "authentication.token.type" this field is present if and only if the authenticating credential is a service account token
# "event.type" informs about what internal system generated the event; possible values are "rest", "transport", "ip_filter" and "security_config_change"
# "origin.address" the remote address and port of the first network hop, i.e. a REST proxy or another cluster node
# "realm" name of a realm that has generated an "authentication_failed" or an "authentication_successful"; the subject is not yet authenticated
# "url.path" the URI component between the port and the query string; it is percent (URL) encoded
# "url.query" the URI component after the path and before the fragment; it is percent (URL) encoded
# "request.method" the method of the HTTP request, i.e. one of GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, HEAD, PATCH, TRACE, CONNECT
# "request.body" the content of the request body entity, JSON escaped
# "request.id" a synthetic identifier for the incoming request, this is unique per incoming request, and consistent across all audit events generated by that request
# "action" an action is the most granular operation that is authorized and this identifies it in a namespaced way (internal)
# "request.name" if the event is in connection to a transport message this is the name of the request class, similar to how rest requests are identified by the url path (internal)
# "indices" the array of indices that the "action" is acting upon
# "opaque_id" opaque value conveyed by the "X-Opaque-Id" request header
# "trace_id" an identifier conveyed by the part of "traceparent" request header
# "x_forwarded_for" the addresses from the "X-Forwarded-For" request header, as a verbatim string value (not an array)
# "transport.profile" name of the transport profile in case this is a "connection_granted" or "connection_denied" event
# "rule" name of the applied rule if the "origin.type" is "ip_filter"
# the "put", "delete", "change", "create", "invalidate" fields are only present
# when the "event.type" is "security_config_change" and contain the security config change (as an object) taking effect

1. Add a log4j2.properties ConfigMap that writes server logs to a file (currently ES only logs to stdout)

cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-log4j2-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  log4j2.properties: |
    status = error

    ######## Server JSON ############################
    appender.rolling.type = Console
    appender.rolling.name = rolling
    appender.rolling.layout.type = ESJsonLayout
    appender.rolling.layout.type_name = server

    appender.rolling_file.type = RollingFile
    appender.rolling_file.name = rolling_file
    appender.rolling_file.fileName = ${sys:es.logs.base_path}${sys:file.separator}${sys:es.logs.cluster_name}_server.json
    appender.rolling_file.filePattern = ${sys:es.logs.base_path}${sys:file.separator}${sys:es.logs.cluster_name}-%d{yyyy-MM-dd}-%i.json.gz
    appender.rolling_file.layout.type = ESJsonLayout
    appender.rolling_file.layout.type_name = server
    appender.rolling_file.policies.type = Policies
    appender.rolling_file.policies.time.type = TimeBasedTriggeringPolicy
    appender.rolling_file.policies.time.interval = 1
    appender.rolling_file.policies.time.modulate = true
    appender.rolling_file.policies.size.type = SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy
    appender.rolling_file.policies.size.size = 128MB
    appender.rolling_file.strategy.type = DefaultRolloverStrategy
    appender.rolling_file.strategy.fileIndex = nomax
    ################################################

    rootLogger.level = info
    rootLogger.appenderRef.rolling.ref = rolling
    rootLogger.appenderRef.rolling_file.ref = rolling_file

    ######## Deprecation JSON #######################
    appender.deprecation_rolling.type = Console
    appender.deprecation_rolling.name = deprecation_rolling
    appender.deprecation_rolling.layout.type = ESJsonLayout
    appender.deprecation_rolling.layout.type_name = deprecation.elasticsearch
    appender.deprecation_rolling.layout.esmessagefields=x-opaque-id,key,category,elasticsearch.elastic_product_origin
    appender.deprecation_rolling.filter.rate_limit.type = RateLimitingFilter

    appender.header_warning.type = HeaderWarningAppender
    appender.header_warning.name = header_warning
    #################################################

    logger.deprecation.name = org.elasticsearch.deprecation
    logger.deprecation.level = WARN
    logger.deprecation.appenderRef.deprecation_rolling.ref = deprecation_rolling
    logger.deprecation.appenderRef.header_warning.ref = header_warning
    logger.deprecation.additivity = false

    ######## Search slowlog JSON ####################
    appender.index_search_slowlog_rolling.type = Console
    appender.index_search_slowlog_rolling.name = index_search_slowlog_rolling
    appender.index_search_slowlog_rolling.layout.type = ESJsonLayout
    appender.index_search_slowlog_rolling.layout.type_name = index_search_slowlog
    appender.index_search_slowlog_rolling.layout.esmessagefields=message,took,took_millis,total_hits,types,stats,search_type,total_shards,source,id

    logger.index_search_slowlog_rolling.name = index.search.slowlog
    logger.index_search_slowlog_rolling.level = trace

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: filebeat-es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  filebeat.yml: |
    filebeat.inputs:
    - type: log
      enabled: true
      paths:
        - /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs/*_server.json
      json.keys_under_root: true
      json.add_error_key: true
      json.overwrite_keys: true
      fields_under_root: true
      fields:
        service.type: elasticsearch
        event.dataset: elasticsearch.server

    output.elasticsearch:
      hosts: ["http://monitoring-es-http.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:9200"]
      index: "filebeat-es-logs-%{+yyyy.MM.dd}"

    setup.template.name: "filebeat-es-logs"
    setup.template.pattern: "filebeat-es-logs-*"
    setup.template.enabled: false
    setup.ilm.enabled: false

    logging.level: warning
    logging.to_stderr: true
EOF

content.thinking:
The ConfigMap update will propagate to the running pods, but it won't restart the filebeat containers. The filebeat containers read the config file once at startup from the ConfigMap. Since ConfigMaps are mounted as a projected volume, the content will be updated, but filebeat needs to be restarted to pick up the new config.

Wait, actually - the filebeat config is mounted as a subPath mount:
```yaml
- name: filebeat-es-config
  mountPath: /etc/filebeat/filebeat.yml
  subPath: filebeat.yml
  readOnly: true
```

With subPath mounts in Kubernetes, ConfigMap changes are NOT automatically propagated to the running pod! The subPath mount creates a bind mount of the specific file at the point in time when the pod started. Changes to the ConfigMap after the pod starts are NOT reflected.
So I need to restart the filebeat pods to pick up the new config. I could delete and recreate them, or use `kubectl rollout restart` on the statefulsets. But restarting the statefulsets would also restart the Elasticsearch pods, which could cause brief cluster disruption. For a statefulset, I could trigger a rolling restart by changing an annotation, which would restart pods one at a time.

The challenge is that I can't restart individual containers within a pod - I'd need to kill the filebeat process and let the kubelet restart it. But since ConfigMap updates via subPath mounts don't propagate automatically, the container would restart with the old config. So I need to restart the entire pods to pick up the ConfigMap changes.

That said, the monitoring is already working - the indices are growing and data is flowing to the monitoring cluster, even with those JSON decode errors. The errors only affect the exception lines, not the core functionality. The task requirements are already met, so this is more of a polish than a necessity. But I'll do a rolling restart anyway to ensure everything works cleanly with the proper configuration.

The error is: "When using the JSON decoder and multiline together, you need to specify a message_key value"

This is a known filebeat 7.x limitation: you can't use both the JSON decoder (`json.*` settings) and multiline together on the `log` input without specifying a `message_key`. The `message_key` tells filebeat which JSON field to use for multiline matching.

But actually, thinking about this more - the multiline processing needs to happen BEFORE JSON decoding. If ES logs are multi-line JSON, then I should collect the multiline text first, then parse the resulting multi-line JSON.

Wait, the error says I need a `message_key` when using both JSON and multiline. 

So the constraint is that in filebeat 7.x, when using the JSON decoder with multiline, I need to specify which JSON field the multiline processor should match against. That means I need to add `json.message_key` to my configuration to tell filebeat which field contains the actual log message text. means filebeat reads each line, decodes it as JSON, then applies multiline rules to the decoded fields—which doesn't work for my case where the raw lines themselves are multi-line JSON. The better approach is to skip JSON decoding at the input level, use multiline to collect the raw lines, and then apply a JSON parser as a processor instead.

I'm configuring filebeat to read the logs with multiline pattern matching that groups lines starting with `{`, then using a decode_json_fields processor to parse the collected message as JSON and place the keys at the root level. This avoids the JSON decoder trying to parse continuation lines that aren't valid JSON on their own.

content.text: Filebeat crashes because you can't use `json.*` and `multiline` together in the log input without `message_key`. Let me fix the config to use `decode_json_fields` processor instead:

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: filebeat-es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  filebeat.yml: |
    filebeat.inputs:
    - type: log
      enabled: true
      paths:
        - /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs/*_server.json
      multiline.type: pattern
      multiline.pattern: '^\{'
      multiline.negate: true
      multiline.match: after

    processors:
    - decode_json_fields:
        fields: ["message"]
        target: ""
        overwrite_keys: true
        add_error_key: true
    - add_fields:
        target: ""
        fields:
          service.type: elasticsearch
          event.dataset: elasticsearch.server

    output.elasticsearch:
      hosts: ["http://monitoring-es-http.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:9200"]
      index: "filebeat-es-logs-%{+yyyy.MM.dd}"

    setup.template.name: "filebeat-es-logs"
    setup.template.pattern: "filebeat-es-logs-*"
    setup.template.enabled: false
    setup.ilm.enabled: false

    logging.level: warning
    logging.to_stderr: true
EOF
